description: Single consortium member, available — trivial pick.
notes: Sanity case. Rules + ground truth agree.

description: One consortium member vs one external, both available — consortium wins.
notes: Consortium weight (0.5) dominates preferred-score gap (0.2).

description: Empty candidate list — no chosen, empty ranking.
notes: Edge case; saga goes to Unfilled.

description: Consortium MEM-A on_loan vs MEM-B available — available wins.
notes: Status weight (0.2) breaks the consortium tie.

description: Two consortium members, MEM-A higher preferred_score — MEM-A wins.
notes: preferred_score (0.2 weight) breaks the otherwise-identical tie.

description: Two consortium members, MEM-B closer — MEM-B wins on proximity.
notes: Proximity (0.1 weight) breaks the otherwise-identical tie.

description: All on_loan consortium — pick the closest one.
notes: Status tied; proximity decides.

description: All external — available beats unknown beats on_loan.
notes: Status hierarchy in absence of consortium signal.

description: Mixed pool, consortium-available outranks external-available.
notes: Even with EXT's near-distance + high preferred + available, consortium weight + status edges out MEM-B and dominates EXT.

description: Reference-only ranks last regardless of consortium membership.
notes: Rules-baseline picks the consortium ref-only because the consortium 0.5 weight beats EXT's status/preferred contribution. This is a known rules quirk; ADR-0014 flags it as a candidate for the LLM tie-breaker — staff would prefer the available external for a circulating loan. expected_chosen reflects the rules-baseline behavior; if the LLM-augmented agent overrides this in PR-2, the ADR threshold will be revisited.

description: Three external, preferred_score breaks tie.
notes: preferred_score sweeps when everything else is equal.

description: Three consortium, status mix — available > unknown > on_loan.
notes: Status hierarchy decides among consortium peers.

description: [GROUND TRUTH ≠ RULES] Two consortium members tied on every visible feature; raw.sla_tier='A' should win over 'B'.
notes: Rules see all-equal and pick by stable-sort input order (MEM-A). Ground truth: SLA tier A is the consortium-policy preference. This is a pure tie-break case where the LLM tie-breaker (PR-2) is expected to win by reading raw metadata.

description: [GROUND TRUTH ≠ RULES] Reciprocity-debt nudges pick away from in-debt member.
notes: Rules tie → stable sort picks MEM-A. Ground truth: MEM-A is in reciprocity debt (we owe them many lends); routing fairly to MEM-B who has lent us less keeps the consortium balance healthy. Tie-break candidate for the LLM.

description: [GROUND TRUTH ≠ RULES] Format match wins over consortium for an article request that requires electronic delivery.
notes: Article request (item.item_kind='article') with electronic-delivery affinity. Post-PR for ADR-0014 addendum: rules baseline now reads item.item_kind + candidate.raw.delivery and applies a +/-0.3 format-affinity adjustment, so rules pick EXT-DIG. Pre-PR: rules picked MEM-A on consortium weight (gap 0.46), LLM never fired because gap > ε.

description: [GROUND TRUTH ≠ RULES] Reliability — consortium MEM-A 60% on-time vs MEM-B 95%.
notes: Rules tie → stable sort picks MEM-A. Ground truth: historical on-time rate is the dominant tie-breaker for routing trust. LLM tie-breaker territory.

description: Far-away consortium beats close external when status is equal.
notes: Consortium weight (0.5) far exceeds proximity gap (0.1).

description: Distance unknown for both — proximity neutralises, status decides.
notes: Both proximities default to 0.5; status breaks the tie.

description: All consortium reference_only — pick most-preferred among them.
notes: Bad-options case; staff still needs an ordering for review. preferred_score wins.

description: Mixed-status consortium, distance-equipped — overall sanity over 4 candidates.
notes: Available consortium > unknown consortium > on_loan consortium > far external. End-to-end ranking sanity check.

description: Single external candidate — no consortium members hold the item.
notes: Sanity case for a non-consortium item pool. Rules and ground truth agree.

description: Five consortium members all available — preferred_score sorts entire field.
notes: Large consortium, identical status/proximity. preferred_score (0.2 weight) sweeps the ranking. Scores: MEM-A 0.93, MEM-B 0.89, MEM-C 0.85, MEM-D 0.81, MEM-E 0.77.

description: Five consortium members identical preferred_score — proximity sorts entire field.
notes: Large consortium, identical status + preferred_score. Proximity (0.1 weight) sweeps. Scores: 0.893, 0.873, 0.853, 0.833, 0.813.

description: Consortium on_loan vs external available — consortium still wins.
notes: Consortium weight (0.5) makes on_loan consortium (score 0.69) beat available external (score 0.35) easily.

description: Consortium members dominate despite externals having proximity advantage.
notes: Consortium weight (0.5) dominates. Within each group proximity sorts: MEM-A(0.887) > MEM-B(0.847) > EXT-A(0.397) > EXT-B(0.360).

description: All candidates have unknown status — preferred_score and consortium weight decide.
notes: No status signal. MEM-A(0.83) > MEM-B(0.73) >> EXT-A(0.35) despite EXT-A's perfect preferred_score — consortium weight dominates.

description: Preferred_score gap of 0.01 — minimal but deterministic rule resolution.
notes: Score delta = 0.002 (from 0.01 preferred gap × 0.2 weight). Rules resolve deterministically even at minimum preferred_score separation.

description: Member at exactly max_distance_km (1500 km) — proximity_score bottoms at 0.0.
notes: MEM-FAR proximity = 1 - 1500/1500 = 0.0; score = 0.80. MEM-NEAR score = 0.887. Full proximity spread is 0.1 — max possible contribution from distance factor.

description: Perfect numeric tie on all four dimensions — stable sort preserves input order.
notes: Both score 0.85. Python sort is stable; MEM-A is listed first in candidates so it retains position #1. Rules baseline and ground truth agree — first-listed member is the consortium-order default.

description: All preferred_score=0.0 — only proximity differentiates consortium peers.
notes: preferred_score zeroed; proximity is the only differentiator. MEM-B(0.780) > MEM-A(0.733).

description: All preferred_score=1.0 — only proximity differentiates consortium peers.
notes: preferred_score saturated at 1.0; proximity is the only remaining differentiator. MEM-B(0.993) > MEM-A(0.920).

description: Three externals, all available — proximity sorts when preferred_score tied.
notes: No consortium members; pure external pool. EXT-B(0.397) > EXT-C(0.367) > EXT-A(0.307).

description: Consortium reference_only outranks all externals despite status penalty.
notes: Consortium weight (0.5) makes reference_only consortium (0.65) beat all externals. Max external score here = 0.31. Known rules quirk noted in routing-010.

description: Two consortium members at equal distance — preferred_score is the tiebreaker.
notes: Identical distance neutralises proximity. preferred_score (0.2 weight) decides. MEM-A(0.927) vs MEM-B(0.827).

description: Six candidates — all four scoring factors interact; verifies full ranking.
notes: Scores: MEM-AVAIL 0.880, MEM-UNK 0.807, MEM-REF 0.777, MEM-LOAN 0.773, EXT-AVAIL 0.499, EXT-UNK 0.360. Interesting: MEM-REF edges MEM-LOAN because MEM-REF has higher preferred+proximity despite zero status score.

description: Status hierarchy isolated — unknown > on_loan > reference_only, all else equal.
notes: Status scores: unknown=0.5, on_loan=0.2, reference_only=0.0. Scores: MEM-A 0.75, MEM-B 0.69, MEM-C 0.65. Isolated status-hierarchy verification.

description: Available+low-preferred vs on_loan+high-preferred — status + preferred_score cross-term.
notes: MEM-AVAIL-LOW(0.78) narrowly beats MEM-LOAN-HIGH(0.77) despite a 0.75 preferred_score gap — available status contributes 0.2 vs on_loan's 0.04, a difference that outweighs 0.15 preferred delta. Confirms status weight dominance over preferred_score at this gap.

description: Eight-candidate mixed pool — comprehensive ranking across all factors.
notes: Scores: MEM-B 0.873, MEM-A 0.847, MEM-C 0.840, MEM-D 0.813, MEM-E 0.737, MEM-F 0.720, EXT-NEAR 0.479, EXT-FAR 0.320. Consortium block sweeps external block. Within consortium: available > unknown > on_loan > reference_only with preferred_score and proximity breaking ties.

description: Consortium on_loan vs externally-co-located external (distance_km=1) — consortium wins.
notes: EXT-NEAR has max preferred_score + max proximity (score 0.500). MEM-LOAN consortium weight alone = 0.5 — at the boundary. Scores: MEM-LOAN 0.690, EXT-NEAR 0.500. Consortium wins by 0.19 even at the extreme external-advantage point.

description: Two consortium + one external — preferred_score breaks consortium tie before external is reached.
notes: Scores: MEM-A 0.91, MEM-B 0.81, EXT 0.43. Even EXT's highest preferred_score can't overcome the 0.5 consortium weight gap. Within consortium, preferred_score sorts correctly.
